01

How to Join

Step 01
Fill Out the Investment Interest Form

Complete our short online form to signal your interest and provide basic qualification information. This is not a binding commitment — it opens the conversation. We review all submissions and will reach out within 48 hours.

Now open · Deadline July 15, 2026
Open Form →
Step 02
Schedule a Call with a Managing Partner

A 30-minute conversation with one of our three managing partners. We will walk through the vehicle, the thesis, your questions, and what active membership looks like in practice. We are selective — this is a circle, not a fund.

By invitation after form review
Step 03
Sign Risk Acknowledgement & Partnership Agreement

All partners sign the Pacto de Socios (Partnership Agreement) and a Risk Acknowledgement. These govern your rights and obligations as a socio of Anza30 S.L. Legal counsel: Pablo Matesanz. Structure: Spanish Sociedad Limitada — not a regulated fund, not under CNMV supervision.

Required before any capital call
Step 04
First Capital Call — 20% of Committed Capital

Once the partnership agreement is signed and the company is constituted, the first capital call is issued: 20% of your committed capital (approximately €4,000 per full unit). Subsequent calls are made throughout the 18-month deployment window as investments are approved by partner vote. You do not need all capital available today.

First call: ~July 30, 2026 · Banco Santander
02

Legal Framework

General notions from the Pacto de Socios, Anza30 S.L. — Barcelona, April 2026. This is not legal advice. Read the full agreement before signing.

Anza30 is constituted as a Spanish Sociedad Limitada (SL) under Spanish corporate law. It is not a regulated investment fund, not an IIC, and is not subject to CNMV authorisation or supervision.

  • Invests exclusively partners' own capital — no public fundraising or third-party asset management
  • All partners are named socios (shareholders) — no anonymous capital
  • Legal counsel: Pablo Matesanz Rodríguez, Barcelona
  • Governing law: Spanish law · Jurisdiction: Courts of Barcelona
  • CNAE code: 6420 — Holding company activities
  • If the company is not constituted by September 30, 2026, the Pacto dissolves automatically with no indemnity obligations

The fund is built around 30 Voting Units at €20,000 each. Units are the fundamental atom of governance and economics.

  • One full unit (€20,000) = one independent vote on all investment decisions
  • Fractional tickets are accepted — smaller commitments are welcome but come with voting syndication: your vote is pooled with another fractional investor to form a complete unit
  • In a €15K/€5K split: the €15K investor is Unit Leader and votes on behalf of the full unit
  • In a €10K/€10K split: both investors designate a Unit Head by mutual agreement; in case of disagreement, the unit abstains
  • Capital raised through up to three successive capital increases, all at the same €600K post-money valuation
  • No single partner may hold 25%+ of the capital, directly or indirectly

Capital is not called all at once. The vehicle operates on a capital call model across an 18-month deployment window. You do not need to have all your capital ready on day one.

  • First capital call: 20% of committed capital (~€4,000 per full unit) at closing, by July 30, 2026
  • Subsequent calls are made as investment decisions are approved by partner vote over the 18-month period
  • Funds wired to Anza30 S.L. at Banco Santander, reference: Ampliación de Capital
  • Target: 8–15 portfolio companies deployed over the window
  • Each investment: €10,000–€100,000 per startup, primarily via convertible loans

Every partner has a voice. Decisions follow a structured majority framework designed for deliberate, not reactive, deployment.

  • New investments: 66% supermajority of votes cast required
  • Portfolio exits: simple majority (50%+1) of votes cast
  • Corporate decisions & structural changes: 66%–75% depending on the matter
  • Quorum: at least 50% of all Voting Units must cast a vote for any result to be valid
  • Voting window: minimum 7 calendar days per decision; silence equals abstention
  • Partners with a direct or indirect interest in a target company must recuse from that vote
  • Votes are anonymous for other partners, but internally traceable by managing partners for compliance

The structure is optimised for cross-border Spain × USA investors under Spanish corporate law.

  • 20% Spanish corporate tax applies only on positive exits (gains only, not on capital returned at cost)
  • Distributions made pro-rata to all partners post-exit and post-tax
  • No additional withholding on LP distributions in Spain
  • Vehicle classified as a sociedad patrimonial — no individual partner holds 25%+ of capital
  • Administrador roles are unpaid by design (gratuity of charge stated in the Pacto and Estatutos)
  • All partners should consult their own tax advisor for personal tax treatment in their jurisdiction

Participaciones are transferable, but subject to right-of-first-purchase by existing partners. This vehicle is highly illiquid by design.

  • Any partner wishing to sell must notify managing partners in writing with proposed buyer, price, and terms
  • Existing partners have 30 days to exercise right of first purchase, pro-rata, on the same terms
  • Unclaimed shares open to a second 10-day round among interested partners
  • If right is not exercised, transfer must occur within 60 days on no worse terms — then the process resets
  • New partners must sign the full Pacto before acquiring any participaciones
  • Target exit horizon: 7 years. Plan accordingly.
  • Amendment to the partnership agreement requires 75% partner approval

Default policy is full distribution of distributable profits, annually, pro-rata to participation.

  • Dividends distributed annually within legal deadlines, pro-rata by capital held
  • Retention of profits requires 66% approval from all socios — allowed for strategic reserve or active pipeline needs
  • Distributions triggered by exits are made punctually as desinversiones occur
  • Annual accounts filed with Registro Mercantil

Risk Acknowledgement

This vehicle is highly illiquid. The target exit horizon is 7 years. There is a real risk of total loss of capital. Interests in Anza30 S.L. are not publicly traded and cannot be easily sold. All investors must sign a Risk Acknowledgement before participating. This document does not constitute legal, tax or financial advice. Consult your own advisors before committing.

Friction never.
